messages.content: hi
Hello! How can I assist you today?

Which of the following company(s) encourage its staff to undertake innovative opportunites outside their current role?
A.
Facebook
B.
3M
C.
Google 
D.
Johnson & Johnson
A, B, C, and D all encourage their staff to undertake innovative opportunities outside their current role.

Which of the following statement(s) about Social Entrepreneurship is/are correct?
A.
It can be broadly defined as innovative activity with a social objective in the for-profit sector.
B.
It can make a significant contribution to communities and society in general by adopting
business models to offer innovative and creative solutions to complex social issues.
C.
Making profit is not an objective for a Social Entreprise.
D.
Its core objective is to create value for citizens, stakeholders, and the wider community by utilizing available resources to exploit opportunities that will generate revenue.

B. It can make a significant contribution to communities and society in general by adopting business models to offer innovative and creative solutions to complex social issues.

D. Its core objective is to create value for citizens, stakeholders, and the wider community by utilizing available resources to exploit opportunities that will generate revenue.

Both statement B and D are correct about social entrepreneurship.

Which of the following statement(s) about Entrepreneurship is/are correct?
A.
In the 17th century, an entrepreneur was an individual who entered into a contractual arrangement with the government to carry out a service or provide acquired products.
B.
In the middle of the 20th century, the idea that entrepreneurs were innovators was established
C.
In the Middle Ages, the term entrepreneur was associated with an actor and an individual who managed large production projects
D.
In the 18th century, the entrepreneur was differentiated from what is today known as the venture capitalist.

B. In the middle of the 20th century, the idea that entrepreneurs were innovators was established.

D. In the 18th century, the entrepreneur was differentiated from what is today known as the venture capitalist.

Both statement B and D are correct about entrepreneurship.

Which of the following statement is correct? 
A.
All are correct.
B.
Psychologists—an entrepreneur is motivated by certain factors—the need to achieve, to experiment,
to acquire, or to control his or her own destiny.

C.
Economists—an entrepreneur brings about innovation and change; he or she utilizes resources,
labor, materials, and other assets in ways that generate greater value and wealth.
 
D.
Managers and Businesspeople—an entrepreneur is an innovator who introduces new ideas for
products, services, processes, or markets. To some business owners/managers, an entrepreneur
seems to be a threat, a major competitor. To other business owners/ managers, an entrepreneur may
be one to unite with, a customer/ client who generates wealth for him or herself and others, identifies
ways to effectively utilize resources, and creates opportunities and employment.

A. All are correct.

All of the statements A, B, C, and D are correct about different perspectives on entrepreneurship.

Which of the following statement(s) about Pubic Sector Entrepreneurship is/are correct?
A.
This can lead to more intensive and extensive innovations in the management of the public sector organization. 
B.
Profit maximisation is its main objective.
C.
The underlying drive for public sector entrepreneurship is the generation of new sources of revenue and the provision of enhanced services to create value for citizens.
D.
Public sector entrepreneurship is a means of achieving more efficient, flexible, and adaptable management in times of uncertainty and change.

A. This can lead to more intensive and extensive innovations in the management of the public sector organization.

C. The underlying drive for public sector entrepreneurship is the generation of new sources of revenue and the provision of enhanced services to create value for citizens.

D. Public sector entrepreneurship is a means of achieving more efficient, flexible, and adaptable management in times of uncertainty and change.

Statements A, C, and D are correct about public sector entrepreneurship. Profit maximization is not the main objective in the public sector, so statement B is incorrect.

Which of the following statement(s) about Social Entrepreneurship is/are correct?
A.
It can be broadly defined as innovative activity with a social objective in the for-profit sector.
B.
Making profit is not an objective for a Social Entreprise.
C.
Its core objective is to create value for citizens, stakeholders, and the wider community by utilizing available resources to exploit opportunities that will generate revenue.
D.
It can make a significant contribution to communities and society in general by adopting
business models to offer innovative and creative solutions to complex social issues.

A. It can be broadly defined as innovative activity with a social objective in the for-profit sector.

C. Its core objective is to create value for citizens, stakeholders, and the wider community by utilizing available resources to exploit opportunities that will generate revenue.

D. It can make a significant contribution to communities and society in general by adopting
business models to offer innovative and creative solutions to complex social issues.

Statements A, C, and D are correct about social entrepreneurship. However, statement B is incorrect. Social enterprises may aim to generate profits, but their primary objective is to create social impact and address social issues.

QUESTION 1

Which of the following is a factor allowing data analytics to become popular in the last decade?
A.
More power machines
B.
Better models
C.
The availability of large-scale data

D.
All of the above

D. All of the above 

All three factors - more powerful machines, better models, and the availability of large-scale data - have contributed to the popularity of data analytics in the last decade.

QUESTION 2

Which of the following is NOT one of the big data’s characteristics?
A.
Volume
B.
Velocity
C.
Validity
D.
Variety

C. Validity

Validity is not typically considered one of the characteristics of big data. The commonly recognized characteristics of big data are Volume, Velocity, and Variety.

The 4 Vs of big data include ____.
A.
viability
B.
validity
C.
vagueness
D.
veracity
D. Veracity

General data forms include ____.

image

text

audio

graph
Yes, the general data forms include image, text, audio, and graph.

What is the goal of machine learning?
A.
Teach machines how to learn
B.
Create new knowledge
C.
Build smart robots
D.
Improve measuring performance by learning from experience for specific tasks
D. Improve measuring performance by learning from experience for specific tasks

Which item does not belong to the unified category of machine learning?
A.
Active learning
B.
Reinforcement learning
C.
Supervised learning
D.
Unsupervised learning

A. Active learning

Active learning is a distinct category of machine learning that involves an algorithm actively selecting which data samples to label or annotate in order to improve the learning process. The other options (B, C, D) belong to the unified category of machine learning.

QUESTION 1

Computer vision is about automatically identifying objects in _____ or _____.
A.
images, videos
B.
images, texts
C.
texts, videos
A. images, videos

Pattern recognition can handle inputs including images, videos, texts and audio files.
 True
 False
True
